name: inkcheck
description: CI for ink interactive-fiction stories — compile checks, exhaustive branch playtesting, runtime-error repro paths, and dead-content detection. Verifies a .ink story the way you'd verify code. Writes no prose.

flags.description: Emit the full report as a single JSON object on stdout. Use this for programmatic consumption.
Treat warnings and unvisited knots as failures (exit 1).
Max choices deep to explore. Default 30.
Max story states to visit. Default 500.
Skip the second pass that shortens repro paths.

exitCodes.0: Clean — compiled and explored with no errors (and, under --strict, no warnings or unvisited knots).
exitCodes.1: Failure — compile errors, runtime errors, or (under --strict) warnings/unvisited knots.
exitCodes.2: Usage error — no story file given or bad arguments.

jsonSchema.compile: { success: bool, issues: [{severity, file, line, message}], errors: int, warnings: int, todos: int }
jsonSchema.stats: { words, knots, stitches, functions, choices, gathers, diverts }
jsonSchema.explore: { statesExplored: int, endingsFound: [{path: string[], finalText, variables}], runtimeErrors: [{message, path}], unvisitedKnots: [{name, location}], truncated: bool }

tools.name: story_stats
tools.description: Word/knot/choice counts plus the full knot list with locations.

tools.name: playtest_story
tools.description: Play one scripted choice path headlessly; returns transcript, tags, variables, errors.

tools.name: explore_story
tools.description: Bounded exhaustive walk: endings, error repro paths, knot coverage.

agentLoop: edit .ink → compile_story → explore_story → fix what it reports → repeat. Use inkcheck as a deterministic oracle to verify a story graph you generated or edited before returning it.
